sources.title: State of Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners — CR 25-16: Fishing Seasons and Regulations, January 1, 2026 – December 31, 2027
sources.quotedText: Ruby Lake National Wildlife Refuge: Season: Open year around in all areas except as posted. Hours: 1 hour before sunrise to 1 hour after sunset. January 1 through June 14 — Daily: 5 trout and 5 black bass. Minimum size for black bass is 10 inches total length. Possession: 10 trout and 10 black bass. June 15 through December 31 — Daily: 5 trout and 10 black bass. Minimum size for black bass is 10 inches total length. Possession: 10 trout and 20 black bass.

sources.title: Ruby Lake NWR — Nevada Department of Wildlife
sources.quotedText: The water supports five game fish species: largemouth bass (averaging ~11 inches), rainbow trout, tiger trout, brown trout, and brook trout. Trout typically exceed 15 inches in size. Stocking Updates: 774 Rainbow Trout, 9.0 in, 10-14-2024; 403 Rainbow Trout, 10.3 in, 04-12-2024; 1446 Rainbow Trout, 10.1 in, 04-09-2024; 1069 Rainbow Trout, 8.1 in, 10-13-2023; 707 Rainbow Trout, 8.1 in, 10-12-2023; 7170 Rainbow Trout, 8.1 in, 10-04-2023; 1404 Rainbow Trout, 9.3 in, 05-02-2023; 1538 Tiger Trout, 8.9 in, 05-02-2023; 3020 Rainbow Trout, 9.4 in, 04-21-2023.

sources.title: Fishing at Ruby Lake National Wildlife Refuge — U.S. Fish & Wildlife Service
sources.quotedText: Boat Restrictions by Season: January 1–June 14: No boats permitted. June 15–July 31: Non-motorized boats and battery-powered electric motors only. August 1–December 31: Non-motorized boats and gas motors (10 hp or less). Fishing Areas: South Marsh; the Collection Ditch; Units 10, 13, 14, 20 and 21; Gravel Pit Pond; Willow Pond; North County Line Pond; and Hidden Springs Pond.

sources.title: Apply & Buy — Nevada Fishing Licenses — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

parameters.note: Open year-round refuge-wide, except in specific sub-areas the refuge posts as closed on-site (see closure rule).

regulations.humanSummary: Ruby Lake National Wildlife Refuge is open to fishing year-round in all areas except as posted.
regulations.verbatimText: Season: Open year around in all areas except as posted.

parameters.note: The refuge is a wetland/marsh complex (South Marsh, the Collection Ditch, numbered dike Units, and several named ponds) rather than one open-water lake; specific sub-areas may be posted closed on-site by the refuge, and access/boat rules vary by area and season.

regulations.humanSummary: Portions of Ruby Lake NWR may be closed to fishing as posted by the refuge; check current on-site signage before fishing.
regulations.verbatimText: Open year around in all areas except as posted.

regulations.humanSummary: Fishing at Ruby Lake NWR is allowed from 1 hour before sunrise to 1 hour after sunset.
regulations.verbatimText: Hours: 1 hour before sunrise to 1 hour after sunset.

parameters.counting_rule: This trout limit is the same in both the January 1–June 14 and June 15–December 31 periods; only the black bass limit changes between periods (see separate bag rules).

regulations.humanSummary: Trout: daily limit 5, possession limit 10, year-round at Ruby Lake NWR.
January 1 through June 14: black bass daily limit 5, possession limit 10, at Ruby Lake NWR.
regulations.verbatimText: January 1 through June 14 — Daily: 5 trout and 5 black bass. ... Possession: 10 trout and 10 black bass.

regulations.humanSummary: June 15 through December 31: black bass daily limit 10, possession limit 20, at Ruby Lake NWR.
regulations.verbatimText: June 15 through December 31 — Daily: 5 trout and 10 black bass. ... Possession: 10 trout and 20 black bass.

parameters.note: Applies to black bass year-round (same minimum size in both bag-limit periods).

regulations.humanSummary: Minimum size for black bass is 10 inches total length at Ruby Lake NWR.
regulations.verbatimText: Minimum size for black bass is 10 inches total length.

parameters.note: Boat access varies by season and sub-area: no boats permitted January 1–June 14; non-motorized boats and battery-powered electric motors only June 15–July 31; non-motorized boats and gas motors of 10 hp or less allowed August 1–December 31. The above summary fields reflect the most restrictive (Jan 1–Jun 14) period — see note/verbatimText for the full seasonal schedule.

regulations.citation: U.S. Fish & Wildlife Service — Fishing at Ruby Lake NWR
regulations.humanSummary: Boat access at Ruby Lake NWR is seasonal: no boats Jan 1–Jun 14; non-motorized boats and electric motors only Jun 15–Jul 31; non-motorized boats and gas motors up to 10 hp Aug 1–Dec 31.
regulations.verbatimText: Boat Restrictions by Season: January 1–June 14: No boats permitted. June 15–July 31: Non-motorized boats and battery-powered electric motors only. August 1–December 31: Non-motorized boats and gas motors (10 hp or less).

parameters.note: Standard Nevada resident/nonresident fishing license required for anglers 12 and older.

regulations.humanSummary: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers age 12 and older; no license required under age 12.
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.
